claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me ask you a few questions to assess your situation.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Does Alex live with you full-time, or do they spend time living with someone else (for example, shared custody arrangements)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me full-time, though they were in care for a short period recently - about a couple of months - but they're back home with me now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. It's good that Alex is back home with you now.

Can I ask - is Alex currently in any form of care, hospital, or detention, or are they fully at home with you now?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is fully at home with me now. They're not in care, hospital, or anything like that anymore.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here? (For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status that allows you to live in the UK?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex? In other words, are you the main person who looks after Alex and makes decisions about their care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I'm the one who looks after Alex and makes all the decisions about their care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or have you been receiving Child Benefit for Alex without any current issues or disputes?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News!

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the key requirements:

✓ **Alex is 8 years old** - under the age limit of 16 (or 20 if in approved education or training)

✓ **You're responsible for Alex** - you're the main person looking after them and making decisions about their care

✓ **Alex lives with you full-time** - they're currently living with you in the UK

✓ **You have the right to reside in the UK** - you meet the residency requirements

✓ **No one else is claiming** - there's no competing claim for Alex

✓ **Alex is not currently in care** - although they were in care temporarily, they're now back home with you full-time

The temporary period Alex spent in care doesn't affect your eligibility now that they've returned to live with you.

### Next Steps

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you were previously receiving Child Benefit and it stopped while Alex was in care, you should contact the Child Benefit Office to let them know Alex is back living with you so your payments can resume.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. Alex lives with you full-time in the UK and is not currently in care, hospital, or detention. You are the person responsible for Alex, you live in the UK with the right to reside here, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex was in care for a short period (a couple of months), they are now back home with you full-time. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit when a child returns to live with you after a temporary period in care. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

The temporary period Alex spent in care does not affect current eligibility as Alex has returned home.
